title: GitOps
summary: GitOps principles, ArgoCD, Flux, pull-based deployments, environment promotion, and multi-cluster management.

sections.content:
## GitOps Principles

GitOps (Weaveworks, 2017) is a set of practices that uses Git as the single source of truth for declarative infrastructure and application deployments.

**Four principles (OpenGitOps):**
1. **Declarative** — desired state described declaratively (K8s YAML, Helm values)
2. **Versioned and Immutable** — all state stored in Git with full history
3. **Pulled Automatically** — software agents continuously reconcile live state with Git
4. **Continuously Reconciled** — agents report drift and self-heal

sections.title: GitOps Pull-Based Deployment
sections.content:
graph LR
    Dev[Developer] -->|git push| AppRepo[App Repo\nsource code]
    AppRepo -->|CI builds image| Registry[Container Registry]
    CI[CI Pipeline] -->|update image tag| ConfigRepo[Config Repo\nHelm values / K8s YAML]
    ConfigRepo -->|ArgoCD detects diff| ArgoCD[ArgoCD\nin cluster]
    ArgoCD -->|reconcile| K8s[Kubernetes Cluster]
    K8s -->|health status| ArgoCD
    ArgoCD -->|sync status| ConfigRepo
    style ArgoCD fill:#4f46e5,color:#fff
    style ConfigRepo fill:#059669,color:#fff

## ArgoCD — Core Concepts

```yaml
# ArgoCD Application — defines what to deploy where
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: order-service-prod
  namespace: argocd
spec:
  project: ecommerce
  source:
    repoURL: https://github.com/org/k8s-config
    targetRevision: HEAD
    path: apps/order-service/production
    helm:
      valueFiles:
        - values-prod.yaml
  destination:
    server: https://prod-cluster.example.com
    namespace: production
  syncPolicy:
    automated:
      prune: true       # delete resources removed from git
      selfHeal: true    # revert manual kubectl changes
    syncOptions:
      - CreateNamespace=true
```

### ArgoCD sync states:
- **Synced** — cluster matches Git ✓
- **OutOfSync** — drift detected (manual change or new Git commit)
- **Progressing** — deployment in progress
- **Degraded** — sync failed or pods unhealthy

## Environment Promotion Strategy

```
Application Repository (source code):
  main branch → CI builds → image:git-sha-abc123

Config Repository (desired state):
  envs/
    dev/
      order-service/values.yaml  (image: sha-abc123 - auto-promoted)
    staging/
      order-service/values.yaml  (image: sha-def456 - auto-promoted after dev tests pass)
    production/
      order-service/values.yaml  (image: sha-ghi789 - manual PR approval required)
```

```yaml
# GitHub Actions — promote to staging after dev tests pass
- name: Promote to staging
  run: |
    cd config-repo
    yq eval '.image.tag = "${{ github.sha }}"' \
      -i envs/staging/order-service/values.yaml
    git commit -am "Promote order-service ${{ github.sha }} to staging"
    git push
  # ArgoCD detects the change and deploys automatically
```

sections.title: Multi-Environment GitOps Flow
sections.content:
graph LR
    Commit[Git commit] -->|CI| Image[Image: sha-abc123]
    Image -->|auto| Dev[Dev Env\nAutomatic]
    Dev -->|tests pass, auto| Staging[Staging Env\nAutomatic]
    Staging -->|PR approval| Prod[Production\nManual gate]
    ArgoCD1[ArgoCD Dev] -->|watches| Dev
    ArgoCD2[ArgoCD Staging] -->|watches| Staging
    ArgoCD3[ArgoCD Prod] -->|watches| Prod
    style Prod fill:#dc2626,color:#fff
    style Dev fill:#059669,color:#fff

## ApplicationSet — Managing Many Apps

ArgoCD ApplicationSet generates Application objects automatically from a template:

```yaml
# Deploy all services to all clusters from a single template
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: ApplicationSet
spec:
  generators:
    - matrix:
        generators:
          - git:
              directories:
                - path: apps/*  # finds all service dirs
          - clusters:           # for each cluster
              selector:
                matchLabels:
                  env: production
  template:
    metadata:
      name: '{{path.basename}}-{{name}}'
    spec:
      source:
        path: '{{path}}'
      destination:
        server: '{{server}}'
```

Result: add a new directory → ApplicationSet automatically creates the ArgoCD Application and deploys it.

sections.question: What does ArgoCD's 'selfHeal' option do?
options: It automatically fixes application bugs found in production
It automatically reverts manual kubectl changes to match the desired state in Git
It updates the container image when a new version is available
sections.explanation: selfHeal=true means ArgoCD continuously compares live cluster state with Git. If someone runs `kubectl edit` or manually patches a resource, ArgoCD detects the drift and reverts it back to the Git-declared state. This enforces Git as the single source of truth and prevents configuration drift from manual interventions.
